parameters-en.Ratio: Determines how much compression is applied to the signal once it exceeds the Threshold. Higher values mean more compression.
parameters-en.Attack: Controls how quickly compression is applied once the signal exceeds the Threshold. Higher values mean a slower attack, which lets the instrument's initial transient sneak through and only compresses the sustained portion of the signal.
parameters-en.Release: Controls how quickly the signal returns to unity gain after it returns below the Threshold.
parameters-en.Lo X Freq: Sets the crossover frequency between the Low and Mid bands.
parameters-en.Hi X Freq: Sets the crossover frequency between the Mid and High bands.
parameters-en.Level: Sets the overall level of the block.
parameters-en.Lo Thresh: Sets the level above which compression is applied to the Low band. Lower values compress more of the signal; higher values compress only louder parts of the signal.
parameters-en.Lo Gain: Adjusts the Low band's level to compensate for the reduced level that results from compression.
parameters-en.Mid Thresh: Sets the level above which compression is applied to the Mid band. Lower values compress more of the signal; higher values compress only louder parts of the signal.
parameters-en.Mid Gain: Adjusts the Mid band's level to compensate for the reduced level that results from compression.
parameters-en.Hi Thresh: Sets the level above which compression is applied to the High band. Lower values compress more of the signal; higher values compress only louder parts of the signal.
parameters-en.Hi Gain: Adjusts the High band's level to compensate for the reduced level that results from compression.
